title: Domain-Adapted Specialists For Pre-Season Testing And Wet/Intermediate Sessions
summary: Official F1 coverage repeatedly notes that pre-season testing pace is distorted by sandbagging, glory runs, and data-collection objectives, while wet/VSC-like conditions materially alter stop economics.
rationale: Official F1 coverage repeatedly notes that pre-season testing pace is distorted by sandbagging, glory runs, and data-collection objectives, while wet/VSC-like conditions materially alter stop economics. That points to distribution shift severe enough for dedicated specialists or sample weighting rather than a single global model.
implementation_hint: Train separate specialists for `Race == Pre-Season Testing`, slick races, and wet/intermediate races; or use one gate feature plus importance weighting so testing rows contribute less to the main dry-race model. Blend by regime at inference using compound/weather proxies already in the table.
expected_effect: Should reduce bias from non-representative testing behavior and from wet-race strategy rules that differ from normal dry degradation logic.
risk: Small subgroup sizes may make specialist models unstable, especially for wet compounds; gains depend on enough regime-specific examples in train.
